reasoning: The Lean signature captures exactly the informal claim. `G` is a finite group via `[Group G] [Fintype G]`, the hypothesis `card G > 2` matches "n = |G| > 2", and the conclusion quantifies universally over subgroups `H` asserting `card H ≠ card G - 1`, which is the standard encoding of "G cannot have a subgroup H with |H| = n-1". The `[Fintype H]` instance is only bookkeeping to allow `card H` to be computed (and is automatically derivable from `[Fintype G]`). Natural number subtraction `card G - 1` is well-behaved here because `card G ≥ 3`. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the conclusion is logically equivalent to the informal assertion.
